name: sort
description: Sorting algorithms for AQL: every well-known sort (comparison, distribution, and joke/educational), driven by composable comparators, exposed as the single `Sort` namespace. Sorts return a NEW sorted List and never mutate their input.

conventions.import: import "./sort.aql"  — no `end` needed on the pinned build; the library imports its own aql:* deps, callers need not
conventions.call: receiver-first, verb in the middle, terminated with `end` (or wrapped in parens): `list Sort.verb comparator end`. The comparator is the trailing argument. Without a terminator the verb swallows the following token.
conventions.comparator: A comparator is a two-argument function value returning a negative / zero / positive Integer when the first item sorts before / equal to / after the second (the sign is all that matters — same contract as the built-in `cmp`).
conventions.passing_comparators: Pass a comparator FROM this namespace bare (`list Sort.quick Sort.by-number end`). Pass your OWN comparator word or the built-in `cmp` with a `/r` suffix (`list Sort.quick mycmp/r end`, `list Sort.quick cmp/r end`).
conventions.no_mutation: Every algorithm returns a NEW sorted List; the input List is never modified. This is the OPPOSITE of an in-place library.
conventions.distribution_sorts: The distribution sorts (counting, pigeonhole, radix-lsd, radix-msd, bucket, bead) take NO comparator and order Integers ascending; radix and bead require non-negative Integers.
conventions.import_resolution: relative to the working directory the script is run from, not the importing file
conventions.errors: failures raise coded errors caught with `do [...] error [...]`; read e.code / e.message. Codes: bad_input (a distribution sort got a non-Integer, or radix/bead got a negative, or the value range is too large), bogo_giveup (Sort.bogo exceeded its shuffle cap).

Comparator.note: A two-argument function value `[b a] -> Integer` returning negative/zero/positive. The library's comparators and combinators are all of this shape.

natural.note: Alphanumeric order: embedded digit runs compare by numeric value, so "file2" < "file10".

reverse.note: Returns a new comparator that reverses `comp` (descending).

by-key.note: Returns a comparator that orders items by the key `keyfn` extracts, compared with the native `cmp`.

comparison_sorts._call: list Sort.<algo> comparator end  ->  List (new, sorted; input unchanged)

notes.merge: Stable; the reference implementation other sorts are checked against.
notes.sort: The recommended general-purpose default (currently stable merge sort).
notes.bitonic: Generalised to any length (not only powers of two).

notes.bogo: Shuffle-until-sorted with a deterministic LCG and a hard cap; raises bogo_giveup past the cap, so use only on tiny inputs.

distribution_sorts._call: list Sort.<algo> end  ->  List (Integers, ascending; NO comparator)

notes.counting: Handles negatives via a min offset; raises bad_input on non-Integers or a value range over 1e8.
notes.radix-lsd: Base-10 least-significant-digit, stable; non-negative Integers only.
notes.radix-msd: Base-10 most-significant-digit, recursive; non-negative Integers only.
